name: KUROI CHOCHO BUNSHIN NO JUTSU (BLACK BUTTERFLY REPLICATION)

description.value:
The user manipulates the shadows into semi solid, weightless forms that most closely resemble butterflies.

This technique is basically a variant of Kage Bunshin no Jutsu that creates solid constructs out of thin air, using no substance or matter as carrier for the user's chakra. Using this technique, the user is able to divide his energy to create several small replicates of birds. While the replications are not capable of sentient thought, they share a bond of sort that allows the user to communicate orders to his constructs; the bond is only one way, though, and the constructs cannot send orders, thoughts or images to the user. As such, the constructs follow the user's instructions to the letter. A Kuroi Chocho is its own creature and if caught in a genjutsu, only the replica will be affected, and not the user and/or the whole group of clone. Anything experienced by the construct will be remembered by the user when the technique expires, although it should be noted that no extra experience or game-wise bonus will be awarded from this, only the knowledge acquired by the clone. Each Kuroi Chocho Bunshin has 1 hp, and will die immediately upon being struck. It shares the user's defense as well, with a -10 penalty, as well as all feats, talents and special abilities (though not templates and/or bloodlines) the user may have, but does not benefit from any temporary bonuses the user may currently have (such as Shodan Kousoku or Tadayou).

A Kuroi Chocho may not stray further than 1 mile/2 level from the user, and may be created in any location that can hold it within 50 feet. It can use any technique the user may have under Rank 2, with the same skills modifier, but doing so requires the construct to borrow the user's chakra directly. In addition, all drawbacks coming from a technique used by a replication not only affects the construct, but also the user. A replica counts as the user if it uses a technique to create clones, and thus they are controlled by the user and his own limit is used.

The Kuroi Chocho is unlike the user in that it carries no items or equipment. Lastly, while the construct can use techniques, it cannot normally perform any activities requiring particular attention, such as sensing chakra, setting off an exploding tag or carrying a serious conversation, or techniques that require hand seals, or half seals. Once the clone reaches 0 hit points, strays too far from the user or the duration expires, it disappears in a puff of smoke. When Sensing Chakra, the clones count as though having half the user's Chakra Pool. Each Kuroi Chocho has a fly speed of 50 feet (as per small vermin) and is subject to all the same sensory limitations as the user.

Creating a Kuroi Chocho not only costs Chakra, but also divides the user's physical energy, which is why this technique is forbidden in the first place. For each construct created by this technique, the user suffers 1d4 points of non-lethal damage.

While the user can freely replace any constructs lost by using this technique more than once,

he cannot control a number greater of constructs than the specified maximum. He can also

choose to dissipate a single construct (or more) of his choice rather than the whole group. He

also cannot control any other sort of clone while using this technique (including simple Bunshins, Kage Bunshins or Tajuu Kage Bunshins). A simple usage of this technique without adding to the cost (3) creates one Kuroi Chocho.

Mastery: Every step of mastery allows the user to create more substantial constructs.

The first step of mastery allows the user to create simple two-dimensional shadows which resemble those of common birds, with only a sense of sight. These constructs must remain in contact with natural or supernatural shadows (excluding other Kuroi Chocho) at all times or lose their form and dissipate.

The second step of mastery allows the user to create more complex, yet still two-dimensional replicas of small, black birds, with the additional sense of hearing. These constructs may briefly (2 round/level) break contact with a natural or supernatural shadow without losing their form.

The third step of mastery allows the user to create complex, three-dimensional replicas with the additional sense of smell. The constructs need not maintain contact with any natural or supernatural shadow to maintain their form, and can move freely through natural lighting.
